zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Whetnall Warner於1434年出生在英國諾福爾可貝斯索普時，他的父親Henry de Wettenhall44歲，母親Cicilie Spayne31歲。他於1451年與Elizabeth Helion在英國艾可利 布普斯亞德結婚。他去世於1488年11月20日在他的家鄉享年54歲。

nameMeanings.name: Warner
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and North German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements war(in) ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’ + heri, hari ‘army’. The name was introduced into England by the Normans in the form Warnier (Old French Garnier). Compare Garner and Werner .
English (of Norman origin): shortened form of Warrener (see Warren 2).

Irish (Cork): when this is not the Anglo-Norman name (see above), an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Murnáin (see Murnane ), found in medieval records as Iwarrynane, from a genitive or plural form of the name, in which m is lenited.

variants.name: Garner
meanings: English (of Norman origin): perhaps occasionally from the Old French personal name Garnier (see Garnier ), but it is exeptionally rare as a personal name in medieval England and no certain evidence has been found for its use as a surname. Compare Warner .

English: from Middle English gern(i)er, garner, gurner, Anglo-Norman French gerner ‘granary’ (Old French grenier, from Late Latin granarium, a derivative of granum ‘grain’). It was probably a metonymic occupational name for someone in charge of the stores kept in a granary.
English and Scottish: commonly shortened form of   Gardner  .

variants.name: Werner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Erwin, Heinz, Gerhard, Ernst, Helmut, Horst, Klaus, Arno, Dieter.

German, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Hungarian, Norwegian, Swedish, and Danish: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’ + heri, hari ‘army’. The surname of German origin is also found in Britain (compare Warner ) and France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Verner .
Germanized or Americanized form of Czech, Hungarian, Swedish, Danish, Croatian, and Slovenian   Verner  , a cognate or variant of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: Whetnall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Whatnall
meanings: English: locative name apparently from Watnall (Notts), which appears as Watenho, 1205 in Place-Names of Notts .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Whetnall Warner was born in 1434, in Besthorpe, Norfolk, England, his father, Henry de Wettenhall, was 44 and his mother, Cicilie Spayne, was 31. He married Elizabeth Helion in 1451, in Helion Bumpstead, Essex, England. He died on 20 November 1488, in his hometown, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Whetnall Warner est né en 1434, à Besthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Henry de Wettenhall, avait 44 ans et sa mère, Cicilie Spayne, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Helion en 1451, à Helion Bumpstead, Essex, Angleterre. Il est décédé le 20 novembre 1488, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 54 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Whetnall Warner(이)가 1434년 영국 노르폴크 베스소에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry de Wettenhall(은)는 44세였고, 그의 어머니 Cicilie Spayne(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1451년 영국 에세스 헬이언 버프스테아드에서 Elizabeth Helion(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1488년 11월 20일 그의 고향에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.
